item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate the way this page is formatted, but you still get an upvote for that harrowing note from Dr. Wondertainment. @_@

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'm not a huge fan of SCPs that require you to read a tale before hand to fill in the spaces but this one… really works for me. i can't explain why. maybe it's the fact that it's a Dr. Wondertainment tragedy story? maybe it's the very dark use of unicorns? dunno, but i definitely enjoyed it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
difference of disposition
hide block

If I was put in the shoes of Sal, I'd be 85% likely strangled myself by noose, only 15% likely to strangle her.

That's why I guess the idea never parsed for me, it wasn't something I would have been likely to consider due to differences in personality and such.

Luckily I am not in Sal's unfortunate position.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler
– hide block

How would you deal with seeing your daughter everyday but she never recognizes you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't for the life of me parse why one body would have been strangled.
Impalement is easy to figure out given angry unicorns have horns and could have theoretically escaped…

But the strangling, I have no clue why Sal's "not-daughter" would be strangled, and it bugs me because it feels like I *almost* have understanding but am not quite there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't know if you're allowed to make changes after the contest deadline, but I feel like this would be helped by making it clearer which PoI was strangled, and which one was impaled. Because I got them backwards, and it didn't make a lot of sense to me, until I straightened them out.

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Not much to add here. Thank you to Malice, aismallard, naepic and mcb for crit on this and/or the tale. Was super fun to work on this, even with all the obdtacles we came across.

More by these authors: http://www.scp-wiki.net/devons-den
http://www.scp-wiki.net/poi-3181-198-file

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can definitely put the story together by reading the SCP and the tale, but as a standalone article, I don't think this conveys the story well. All the components of the narrative are kind of scattered between sections and you're relying on the reader to fill in several blanks. What is presented isn't explored very deeply.

I like that Isabel is sad about her actions, that felt real, but the actions felt kind of out of character for her.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
This one was a journey, too. I think I'm starting to get the hang of this CSS thing- the cards and that letter both look GREAT!

This was an awesome piece, co-authored with Crashington for the Collab-Con. We both had a lot of trouble- but also a lot of fun- writing it. What a great team!
